signals.title: AUD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + US-Iran Deal
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7046 — US-Iran MoU signing in Geneva and Hormuz optimism drive a 3.24% collapse in WTI oil, stripping the commodity-linked AUD of its primary support. The G10 scorecard confirms a strong bilateral theme with USD outperforming peers by +0.521% while AUD lags at -0.885%. Despite a -60bp AUD carry advantage (RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75%), the 1.406% G10 spread and negative CESI (-9 misses) for AUD signal a structural shift toward USD dominance as geopolitical risk premia deflate.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Vol Premium
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.11 — price holds above the 160.00 psychological barrier previously associated with BoJ verbal warnings. Options markets are pricing extreme caution with a +3.8% Vol Risk Premium (IV 7.2% vs HV30 3.4%) and a moderate -1.09 25d RR put skew, signaling institutional demand for JPY protection despite the +300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage. JPY COT LF net short -105,136 remains crowded; a sustained break above 160.00 increases the probability of direct intervention.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Positioning + Vol + CB Bias
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3416 — COT LF net long +27,022 (65.6% long) faces a massive +5.0% Vol Risk Premium (IV 11.0% vs HV30 6.0%), the highest in the G10 space. While Deutsche Bank notes hawkish risks for the BoE, the -0.81 25d RR put skew suggests the options market is fading the speculative long position. With the Fed-BoE carry neutral at 0bp, GBP/USD remains vulnerable to a long-unwind if the BoE holds a neutral stance at the June 18 meeting.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Retail Divergence + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3981 — retail sentiment is at an extreme 92% short, diverging from the COT LF net short -49,052 (CAD weakness). This institutional vs retail divergence occurs as the USD/CAD vs WTI correlation breaks (z = -1.57σ), with the pair rising despite the oil collapse. The +150bp USD carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25%) and positive USD CESI (+30) support further upside; retail capitulation risk is high as the pair tests the 1.4000 handle.

signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Hike vs USD Carry
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1579 — ECB's Kocher reaffirms the hiking cycle to stabilize inflation, yet the pair remains capped by a +150bp USD carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.25%). EUR COT LF net short -22,320 suggests specs are already positioned for weakness, while a +2.8% VRP indicates the market is paying a premium for protection. Upside remains structurally limited by the real rate differential (USD +1.31% vs EUR +0.03%) until the Fed signals a pivot.
